sourceMovie.title: Resident Evil: Vendetta
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Resident Evil: Vendetta (2017) as a action theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The action and animation blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Resident Evil: Degeneration
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Resident Evil: Vendetta's action stayed with you, Resident Evil: Degeneration (2008) delivers the same sensation through an entirely different story. The animation through-line is the real common thread between them.

recommendations.title: Resident Evil: Death Island
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Where Resident Evil: Vendetta excels in action, Resident Evil: Death Island (2023) doubles down on animation to comparable effect. The tonal similarities run deep enough that watching both back-to-back feels intentional.

recommendations.title: Resident Evil: Damnation
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Resident Evil: Damnation (2012) is the natural next step after Resident Evil: Vendetta: same action DNA, different perspective. The dark streak and animation elevate it well above a simple genre exercise.

recommendations.title: Dante's Inferno: An Animated Epic
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Fans of Resident Evil: Vendetta who respond to its action will find Dante's Inferno: An Animated Epic (2010) immediately familiar. Both films share a animation sensibility without ever feeling like carbon copies of each other.

recommendations.title: Deathstroke: Knights & Dragons - The Movie
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: There's a reason Resident Evil: Vendetta fans keep landing on Deathstroke: Knights & Dragons - The Movie (2020) — the action is unmistakably related even when the plots diverge. Both films operate on the same emotional frequency, especially in their third acts.

recommendations.title: Resident Evil
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Resident Evil draws from the same action well as Resident Evil: Vendetta, channeling science-fiction in ways that feel unmistakably related. The 2002 film makes a natural companion watch — similar in spirit but distinctly its own.

recommendations.title: Resident Evil: Apocalypse
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Resident Evil: Apocalypse (2004) captures the action appeal of Resident Evil: Vendetta without simply imitating it. The science-fiction streak running through both films is what puts Resident Evil: Apocalypse at the top of any "if you liked…" shortlist.

recommendations.title: Cowboy Bebop: The Movie
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The action appeal of Resident Evil: Vendetta — that uplifting edge, the animation — translates cleanly into Cowboy Bebop: The Movie (2001). Different setting, same essential satisfaction.

recommendations.title: GANTZ:O
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The filmmakers behind GANTZ:O (2016) clearly understood what makes action work — the same quality that keeps Resident Evil: Vendetta rewatchable. Expect the same uplifting rhythm, executed with a fresh angle.

recommendations.title: [REC]²
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: [REC]² (2009) is the kind of film Resident Evil: Vendetta fans discover and immediately send to their group chat. The horror overlap is real, but it's the dark emotional pull that seals the recommendation.

faq.question: What should I watch after Resident Evil: Vendetta?
faq.answer: Try Resident Evil: Degeneration, Resident Evil: Death Island, Resident Evil: Damnation—each shares DNA with Resident Evil: Vendetta while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Resident Evil: Vendetta?
faq.answer: Resident Evil: Vendetta (2017) is best described as Action, Animation, Adventure, Science Fiction, Horror. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Resident Evil: Vendetta part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Resident Evil (Animated) Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
